name: Guowang

overview.value: Guowang, officially the Satellite Internet Series Satellites, is a Chinese low-Earth-orbit satellite internet constellation created by China Aerospace Science and Industry Corporation (CASIC) to provide worldwide internet coverage. The project was started in 2022 as a rival to Starlink and plans to be constituted of over 13,000 satellites by the project's end. The megaconstellation is managed by China SatNet (CSCN).

orbit.value: LEO, two shells at ~1,175 km (86.5° incl.) and ~1,156 km (50.0° incl.)

base.reason: SpaceNews (trade press) states 'the near-term target for Guowang is to have 400 satellites in orbit by 2027'; no first-party China SatNet status statement found

notes: Added in the audit Phase 3 backfill (2026-07-08), verify-then-add. sats_launched_total updated 2026-07-09 to 177 per Wikipedia's launch-log table (Hulianwang 169-177, SatNet LEO Group 22, 2026-06-17); note the same Wikipedia page's prose summary elsewhere still reads 168 as of this write. sats_active_claimed stays null: no source distinguishes a current-active count from cumulative launched. Weekly maintenance (2026-07-13): re-fetched Wikipedia; the launch-log table still shows 2026-06-17 (Hulianwang 169-177) as the most recent entry, confirming 177/2026-06-17 remain current, while the article's separate prose summary paragraph now reads a stale 198 (dated to an October 2025 snapshot, itself inconsistent with the more granular launch-log table). Kept the launch-log-derived 177 rather than the prose summary; this is a known same-metric contradiction within one Wikipedia page, flagged again for Florian, not resolved by overwriting.

events.headline: China's SASAC creates China Satellite Network Group to run Guowang (per SpaceNews)
China launches first ten Guowang constellation satellites on Long March 5B
